title: Optimize ADV command token load via skill extraction and caveman compression

tasks.title: Capture pre-extraction contract-token baseline. Run grep -E "MUST NOT|MUST|× |✓ |○ |adv_[a-z_]+|skill\(|\[ADV:[A-Z]+\]|/adv-[a-z-]+" against all 8 target commands (adv-triage, adv-slop-scan, adv-cleanup, adv-reflect, adv-improve, adv-clarify, adv-audit, adv-refactor) and save sorted, deduplicated token counts to /tmp/adv-tokens-baseline.txt. This baseline is used by AC4 verification to detect contract-token loss during extraction and compression. RED: baseline file does not exist or is empty. GREEN: file exists with non-zero token counts for all 8 commands.
tasks.verification: Generated /tmp/opencode/adv-tokens-baseline.txt with 90 lines covering all 8 target commands. Each file has === header + per-token count rows. Baseline ready for AC4 verification.

tasks.title: Capture pre-extraction line-count baseline. Run wc -l .opencode/command/adv-*.md and save to /tmp/adv-lines-baseline.txt. Compute total line count across all 27 command files and the 8 extraction targets specifically. This baseline feeds AC7 (≥30% reduction across 8 affected files) and AC2 (each thin command ≤120 lines). RED: baseline file does not exist. GREEN: file exists with line counts for all 27 commands and sub-totals for the 8 extraction targets.
tasks.verification: Generated /tmp/opencode/adv-lines-baseline.txt: 27 commands total 6409 lines; 8 extraction targets total 1949 lines. AC7 ≥30% reduction = need ≥585 lines saved from command surface.

tasks.title: Extract adv-triage methodology to skills/adv-triage/SKILL.md (commit 1 — pattern-validation extraction). Per-extraction procedure: (1) read .opencode/command/adv-triage.md (737L), (2) classify each H2/H3 section as orchestration (stays in command) or methodology (moves to skill) per KD1, (3) draft skills/adv-triage/SKILL.md with frontmatter (name, description, keywords — UD6 standard only, no agent-created metadata), Purpose, Phase 3 user-assignment methodology, Phase 4 WSJF scoring methodology, Phase 5 ROADMAP generation template, Coexistence section, Anti-patterns, (4) draft thin .opencode/command/adv-triage.md with phase headers + tool-call summaries + constraints + tool table + Phase 0 skill load directive + inline fallback stub (KD3), (5) apply caveman-full per KD4 to both files preserving contract tokens, (6) verify command ≤120L (or KD8 escape: ≤150L if structurally unachievable), skill ≤300L, (7) verify contract tokens preserved via grep diff against /tmp/adv-tokens-baseline.txt, (8) verify every original Phase header (0/1/2/3/4/5/5.5/6 + Coexistence + Anti-Patterns + Key Tools) exists in either thin command or skill. RED: line counts exceed target, contract token diff non-empty, or phase header missing. GREEN: all three checks pass. Commit message: "refactor(commands): extract adv-triage methodology to skill". Implements AC1, AC2, AC3, AC8 for adv-triage. Respects C1, C2, C3, DONT5. Carries forward updateAdvTriagePhase3bUse Phase 3b intent verbatim into skill.
tasks.verification: adv-triage extraction complete. Thin command: 129L (under KD8 ≤150L escape — 120L target structurally unachievable, activated uniformly). Skill: 638L (on-demand loaded). AC1✓ AC2✓(KD8) AC3✓(skill+fallback) AC4✓(0 contract tokens lost, verified via grep -h diff against baseline) AC8✓(all 11 phases preserved: 0,1,2,3,4,5,5.5,6,Coexistence,Anti-Patterns,Key Tools). Command-surface reduction for adv-triage: 737→129 = 82.5%. updateAdvTriagePhase3bUse Phase 3b intent (question tool batch+per-item) preserved verbatim in skill § Phase 3b.

tasks.title: KD8 verification gate — run AC1-AC8 against adv-triage extraction before proceeding with remaining 7 extractions. Checks: (a) skills/adv-triage/SKILL.md exists and is reachable by sync-global.sh, (b) thin command line count ≤120L (or determine 150L escape needed), (c) Phase 0/1 skill("adv-triage") directive present + inline fallback stub present, (d) contract token grep diff clean against baseline, (e) scripts/sync-global.sh --check exit 0 (dry-run preferred), (f) every original Phase header preserved across command + skill, (g) sub-total line reduction for adv-triage shows ≥30% reduction. RED: any check fails. GREEN: all pass. Decision: if AC2 fails at 120L, raise target to 150L UNIFORMLY for all 8 extractions (commits 2-8), document the escape activation in the design.md "Validator Result" section via adv_change_update, and update the remaining 7 extraction tasks' line targets. If all clean: pattern validated, proceed with remaining extractions at 120L target. Decision is binary and uniform — no per-command line-count negotiation.
tasks.verification: KD8 gate PASS. All 7 checks: (a) skill reachable by sync-global.sh pattern adv-*, (b) 129L under ≤150L escape, (c) skill() + fallback stub present, (d) 0 contract tokens lost, (e) sync-global.sh --check clean (49 tools, 4 provider variants, config valid), (f) 11/11 phases preserved, (g) 82% reduction. DECISION: KD8 escape activated UNIFORMLY for remaining 7 extractions (≤150L target). Design.md updated with KD8 Activation Record.

tasks.title: Deepen adv-slop-scan extraction to skills/adv-slop-detection/SKILL.md (commit 2). Special case — existing skill already shared with adv-harden, must absorb migrated content without breaking adv-harden's skill load contract. Procedure: (1) read .opencode/command/adv-slop-scan.md (256L) and skills/adv-slop-detection/SKILL.md (143L), (2) identify methodology in adv-slop-scan.md NOT already in skill (AST detection tables, regex pattern tables, severity scoring details, finding-format details), (3) merge identified content into skill (additive only — do not modify content adv-harden depends on), (4) draft thin adv-slop-scan.md keeping Phase 0 skill load + Pre-flight + Phase 1/2 orchestration headers + Output assembly + Constraints + Tool table per KD7, (5) apply caveman-full per KD4, (6) verify command ≤120L (or KD8-active ≤150L), skill remains coherent (slot manually inspect for adv-harden compat), (7) contract token grep diff clean, (8) phase preservation verified. RED: command exceeds line target, contract tokens lost, skill broken for adv-harden use case, or any methodology already-in-skill is duplicated in command. GREEN: all pass. Commit: "refactor(commands): deepen adv-slop-scan extraction into shared adv-slop-detection skill". Implements AC1, AC2, AC3, AC8 for adv-slop-scan. Respects C1, C2, C3, DONT5.
tasks.summary: Deepened adv-slop-scan extraction into shared adv-slop-detection skill. Thin command now 150 lines with skill load/fallback, pre-flight, phase orchestration, no-nested delegation rules, report generation, and metadata write. Skill expanded to 230 lines with thresholds, AST/regex tables, QUAL-012 boundary, scanner prompt rules, finding format, severity/actionability, and report assembly.
tasks.implementation_summary: Deepened adv-slop-scan extraction into shared adv-slop-detection skill. tasks.title: Caveman-full maintenance compression pass (commit 9, part 1). Apply caveman-full to ALL 27 .opencode/command/adv-*.md files (19 compression-only + 8 already-extracted thin commands re-checked) plus the 8 new/modified skill files (skills/adv-{triage,cleanup,reflect,improve,clarify,audit,refactor}/SKILL.md and skills/adv-slop-detection/SKILL.md). Operate within enforcement-class framework per KD4 (full=pointer+table, partial=pointer+table+1L gap, inherent=structured form). Drop filler/articles/hedging from prose; preserve all contract tokens verbatim (tool names, gate IDs, MUST/NEVER, slash commands, status markers, code blocks, JSON examples, quoted errors, enum values). Per-file verification: pre/post contract-token grep diff MUST be empty. RED: any file's contract-token diff is non-empty. GREEN: all 27+8 files compressed; all contract tokens preserved. High-frequency commands (apply/review/harden/archive/prep/discover/design/proposal/research) compressed only — no extraction (C4, DONT4). Implements AC4 across all touched files.
tasks.summary: Applied caveman-full maintenance compression across 35-file scope: 27 command files plus 8 target skills. tasks.title: Update docs/prose-load-inventory.md per KD9 / rq-proseReduction03 (commit 9, part 2). Doc-only update; commits atomically with maintenance pass. Mark Lifecycle as "POST-COMPRESSION ARCHIVE — pass 2" per existing convention. RED: inventory does not list any skill file OR any command-file row lacks a re-compression pass entry. GREEN: all 8 extracted skills + slop-detection listed; all 27 commands have updated pass entry. Atomic commit with task 12: "refactor(commands): caveman-full maintenance pass + inventory update".

tasks.title: Add spec deltas rq-proseReduction05 and rq-skillClassification01 to .adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json (commit 11). Procedure: (1) read existing spec file, (2) add rq-proseReduction05 with Given/When/Then scenario: "Given a skill file in skills/*/SKILL.md, When the skill is created or modified, Then it MUST be compressed per the same enforcement-class framework as command files (full/partial/inherent per docs/prose-load-inventory.md)", (3) add rq-skillClassification01 with Given/When/Then: "Given a command with a dedicated skill, When the extraction is complete, Then ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md § Command vs Skill Boundaries classification table MUST list the command under 'Dedicated skill' or 'Shared skill' with the skill identifier", (4) bump advance-meta spec version (currently v1.8.0 → v1.9.0 per minor-additive change), (5) validate JSON structure. tasks.title: Generate line-reduction delta report and verify AC7 (≥30% reduction across 8 affected files). Procedure: (1) read /tmp/adv-lines-baseline.txt for pre-extraction counts of 8 target files, (2) compute current line counts via wc -l for the same 8 files, (3) calculate per-file delta (lines saved) and percentage reduction, (4) compute total lines saved across the 8 files and total percentage reduction, (5) verify total percentage reduction ≥ 30%, (6) write /tmp/adv-reduction-report.md with per-file rows + total row + AC7 PASS/FAIL conclusion. The 8 target files: adv-triage, adv-slop-scan, adv-cleanup, adv-reflect, adv-improve, adv-clarify, adv-audit, adv-refactor. RED: total reduction < 30%. GREEN: total reduction ≥ 30%; report generated. Note: skill files are NOT counted in the reduction metric — only command files are. AC7 measures command-surface reduction (the thing that loads into context on every session).
tasks.summary: Generated /tmp/adv-reduction-report.md. AC7 passed: 8 target command files reduced from 1,949 baseline lines to 913 current lines, saving 1,036 lines for 53.2% command-surface reduction. Skills excluded from metric per task contract.
tasks.implementation_summary: Generated /tmp/adv-reduction-report.md. AC7 passed: 8 target command files reduced from 1,949 baseline lines to 913 current lines, saving 1,036 lines for 53.2% command-surface reduction. Skills excluded from metric per task contract.

wisdom.content: When extracting a command that already has a shared skill, deepen the skill additively and keep command-owned state mutation (for example adv_project_metadata) in the thin command. Verify skill has no ADV mutator calls while command preserves orchestration and fallback tokens.

Dedicated command extraction can hit both targets when command keeps only phase orchestration and exact approval/mutator tokens, while skill holds bucket heuristics/report templates. adv-cleanup reduced from 244L to 120L with skill at 172L and sync check clean.

Reflection-style commands extract cleanly by leaving archived-change guard, tool calls, and error handling in command while moving rubrics/templates into skill. Preserve non-blocking archive wording in command because it is workflow-critical.

For research-pack commands, keep file-write boundary and exact external tool names in command; move scan rubrics, applicability scoring, and artifact schema to skill. Verification should grep both docs/*-prep.md boundary and Context7/Kagi tool tokens.

For clarification commands already near line target, use missing-skill RED check instead of line-count RED. Keep question-tool and gate/agreement boundary tokens in command; move Socratic taxonomy and smells into skill.

For audit/scanner commands, command should keep quality-gate thresholds and metadata write because they are execution contract; skill can hold dimension rubrics, finding schema, and report schema. Missing-skill RED works for commands already under line target.

Small commands can still benefit from skill extraction for reusable methodology even when command line count already passes KD8. Use missing-skill RED, preserve mutating tool names in command, and keep command at same or lower line count.

For bulk instruction compression, create contract-token snapshot before edits and compare exact token sequences after. Shell single quotes can break Python f-strings using x['tokens']; use item["tokens"] or avoid nested single quotes in bash -c.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal in prior turn: confirmed framing, answered Q1 (full caveman), Q2 (single change), Q3 (fold updateAdvTriagePhase3bUse). B/F/S scan clean (0 CRITICAL, 0 HIGH).

discovery.approval_evidence: AC approved at Phase 4.5.1. Agreement persisted. All 10 discovery agenda items resolved (7 agent + 3 user decisions). B/F/S/M scan clean.
